reviews.review_description:
I am sitting here and trying to gather my emotions. I feel humbled (if that’s the right word)... Not sure how to express myself after being in such an intimate environment. I don’t think one can explain it. 

You must see and watch.  I believe this should be in all the classrooms around the world, so young people can bear witness to the powerful truths of the people who shared and bared their souls.  Maybe this will touch the core of a person and change will happen.  

One day, as Mr. Green said.. it won’t be necessary, but now it is necessary and tomorrow no doubt. 

Thank you for creating this thank you for allowing me to watch and listen. Brilliant.
It should be compulsory in schools. This shall not be forgotten and we should learn from history.

I kind of don't know where to start. So instead, I'll just say that I thought this was extremely well done. The subject matter is of course what resonates most, but there is much here that resonates with me artistically too. The use of the mirrors in the diner was wonderfully done. 

My only criticism is that I wish it were longer, so that the subject could be more fully discussed and explored and so that the medium doesn't overshadow the message.

More please

VR is the greatest empathy machine and has the power to put you in a situation you otherwise would not have. Felix & Paul always produces incredible work, but this is by far their most powerful and inspiring project, giving you a visceral glimpse into what it's like being black in America. As a minority growing up in America, I could definitely relate to the racism and ignorance, but as I'm not black American, I have not experienced the fear. And it's tragic that even with the progress we've made as a society, fear is still prevalant, and almost made acceptable in the current political climate. 

This film is not just a must-see for VR, but a must-see, period. This is what creating in VR is all about.
I absolutely hate this it won't even install on here. THIS IS STUPID!

developer_response.body: Hi Madyson, thanks for letting us know about this. Could you send an email to QA@felixandpaul.com explaining the issue and letting us know what headset you're using? Our team will be able to help from there. Thanks!

reviews.review_description: A powerful and important message, should be required viewing. My only complaint is that the app should provide subtitles.
With the times that we face now, I think this is important for all to experience.
Solid. Engaging .

This is informative I suppose if you never went to school or watched a documentary, but I can honestly say there are a lot of things to do in VR and this is not something I'll be doing. I've been oppressed my whole life too but I'm not going to make everyone experience my life simulator. The title itself as well as the concept and intentions bother me. But that's just my opinion.
Wow the 360 view is awesome..highly recommended
Nice
An amazing, heartbreaking, educational experience
I am a white rapper who should have been born as a black man. This is awesome, dude get it.
Can't review as this app refuses to use my mobile network. It gives error saying it requires wifi connection.

One of the best artistic pieces I’ve experienced on the topic of race in the USA. The cinematic quality of ‘being there’ was both innovative and deeply emotional. I look forward to seeing more pieces like this in the future.
My only complaint is that it was too short. So well done and made great use of the oculus !

I did not know the circumstances surrounding Tamir Rice, unfortunately the media does not give details. His mother looks tatted up, not the normal motherly loving figure we commonly associate with, and appears to shed no emotion for her loss, however we can say there was gross negligence of the office post-incident.

I was raised in California by loving parents whom taught to respect all people regardless of color. We're Christians, and always willing to have a shoulder to cry on, give empathy, and love one another. Now at age 43, I can look back and honestly say I have rarely received a compliment from a Black person. Although I have given them myself, I did not receive them back. I was often told "I'm privileged, and rich" even though my low-middle class family struggled as far back as I can remember. I take good care of myself but for some reason I am "on steroids or taking unnatural things because there's no way Whitey lifts heavier than me." Or my personal favorite, "you owe reprarations for owning slaves." I hate to break it to you, my family was too dirt poor to afford slaves and it contradicts our faith regardless.

The old woman mentioned she fear the police in America. I fear Black men for the most part. I am sorry to say but you've painted quite the masterpiece in my life. Always playing the victim, weapons, drugs, rape...my favorite was welcoming my sister's exhusband to the family. We all had an open mind, but 6 months later we all found out he decided to open several joint credit cards forging my sister's signature. He racked up $60k in stolen funds to treat his exgf. She got that marriage annulled.

I always try to keep things in perspective that we're all trying but it can get difficult when there is not much positive coming from the other side.  I hope and pray that will not always be the case and everyone can be bound in love.

Great use of the 360 3d.    And the message is great.  

Very nice (and FREE)
What a great film.  I wish it had been longer.

I never write reviews about anything. Ever. I just don’t care enough and sadly, I rarely have time to use my Oculus these days. 

But this...worth every second of my time! 

This piece is exceptionally well done and so important. I would recommend to everyone ! I am a African American man and I learned things that I didn’t even know about my own history. This piece is amazing and congratulations to all involved with putting this together.
